NYC GHOST NEIGHBORHOODS FULL REPORT · 264 ZONES

// 2011-01 → 2024-12 · yellow + green + FHV + HVFHS

Ghost Neighborhoods of NYC

2.76B for-hire trips across 168 months and 264 taxi zones — a data-first X-ray of which parts of NYC quietly stopped getting picked up. Computed across 371 monthly trip-records files from the NYC TLC by a 371-way parallel Burla map in 14.5s.

ghost (2)cooling (56)stable (12)warming (57)emergent (135)

The entire NYC for-hire fleet, month by month

2011-01 → 2024-12 · single line, one point per month, every taxi + every rideshare pickup.

Ghost Zones

Zones that peaked during the taxi era and never came back. Ranked by current trips/month as a fraction of their all-time monthly peak.

Newborn Zones

Zones where for-hire pickups barely existed in their first two years of observation, but are now pulling meaningful volume. Ranked by growth ratio.

Resurrected Zones

Zones that had a historic peak, a deep trough, and a measurable recovery from that trough.

mass extinction event

12 of 12 resurrected zones bottomed in the same month: 2020-04.

When the data is allowed to speak, the pandemic shows up as a single visible knife-cut across NYC’s busiest neighborhoods.
#1 · Queens · bottomed 2020-04

LaGuardia Airport

peaked 2019-05 at 609.9K/mo · now 502.8K/mo · 53.62M trips total · active 2011-01–2024-12
trough 10.7K/mo → now 502.8K/mo
